summary: Iteration 2 backend testing complete: webhook CRUD, internal/inbound endpoint with shared-secret auth, /messages/send-media multipart, public API media_url validation, /api/messages direction filter, HMAC algorithm format. All 46/46 pytest tests passed (29 carryover from iteration_1 + 17 new for iteration_2). Backend service healthy; watchdog & supervisor reload cleanly without crash.

minor.issue: $unset removes fields from doc but UserOut still returns null (acceptable). Confirmed via /auth/me — fields come back as None. Non-issue but worth noting that webhook_url/secret keys are removed from the document rather than set to null.

Always returns {sent:true} even when delivery fails (by design — async fire-and-forget). Backend logs WARNING on delivery failure which is correct, but a caller cannot tell from this endpoint whether the webhook actually reached the target. Consider returning latency/status_code from a synchronous test fire.
Returns 200 with {ok:false, reason:'session not found'} — returning 404 would be more conventional for unknown session_id. Current behavior matches spec.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 944 lines — approaching the 700-line guideline ceiling. Recommend splitting into routers/modules (auth.py, admin.py, sessions.py, messages.py, webhooks.py, public_api.py, internal.py) before adding more endpoints.

/api/messages/send-media reads the entire upload into memory before checking size — for a 25MB cap this is fine, but the order should be reversed (use UploadFile.size or stream-and-bail) to avoid OOM under attacker-controlled large uploads. Currently any file is fully buffered before the 25MB check rejects it.

Public API /api/v1/messages downloads media_url with httpx without enforcing a max-size limit on the response body. A malicious media_url could stream a multi-GB payload. Recommend adding a Content-Length pre-check and a streaming size guard during download.
fire_webhook silently swallows all exceptions — consider counting failures per-user (e.g., to disable webhook after N consecutive failures, classic 'pause' pattern).

InboundIn model: 'has_media' is bool but the Node service may send richer media metadata (mimetype, url, file name). Consider extending the model so inbound media events can be persisted with file metadata, mirroring outbound /send-media.

wa_supervisor watchdog: no test exercised it directly (would require killing the Node process), but startup/reload logs show no crash and Node service comes up healthy on every reload — looks correct.
Direction filter on /api/messages silently ignores invalid values (anything other than 'inbound'/'outbound') instead of returning 400. Minor UX nit.
test_credentials: admin@wapihub.com / admin123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md & /app/backend/.env). INTERNAL_SECRET read from /app/backend/.env at runtime by tests.
seed_data_creation: Tests register fresh TEST_customer_* accounts each run (cleaned up via session teardown), create transient WA sessions then delete them. No persistent pollution.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration_2 features verified at API level. Real WhatsApp QR scan still out-of-scope (no device pairing tested). HMAC signature was verified at the algorithm level only — to verify end-to-end signing, future agents could spin up a tiny aiohttp/Flask listener as a webhook target inside pytest, set webhook_url to it, trigger /me/webhook/test, and recompute hmac_sha256 over the captured body to compare against X-Wapihub-Signature header. Watchdog respawn behavior was not directly tested (would need to SIGKILL the Node process and observe restart) — could be added with a fixture that calls `pkill -f wa-service/server.js` and waits for /api/health to flip back to wa_service:'ok'.
